title: Better Quantum Computing Stock to Buy: IonQ vs. Rigetti
author: Keithen Drury
description: IonQ emerges as the superior quantum computing pure-play stock compared to Rigetti Computing, leveraging its trapped-ion technology that achieves 99.99% 2-qubit gate fidelity and generating significantly higher revenue ($65M in Q1 vs. Rigetti's $4.4M). While both companies pursue different quantum computing approaches, IonQ's superior accuracy, greater computational capacity, and stronger market traction position it as the better investment, though diversification through quantum computing ETFs is recommended given sector risks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Superior trapped-ion technology with 99.99% 2-qubit gate fidelity, significantly higher revenue growth (755% YoY to $65M), greater qubit capacity (256-qubit system), and stronger market adoption with multiple partnerships and system sales. Explicitly recommended as the better buy.

Superior trapped-ion technology with 99.99% 2-qubit gate fidelity, significantly higher revenue growth (755% YoY to $65M), greater qubit capacity (256-qubit system), and stronger market adoption with multiple partnerships and system sales. Explicitly recommended as the better buy.

Uses superconducting approach with 99.1% 2-qubit gate fidelity (below commercial viability threshold), significantly lower revenue ($4.4M in Q1), and substantially smaller market presence. While not dismissed entirely, positioned as lagging behind IonQ with potential for future breakthroughs.

title: Is Rigetti Computing Stock a Buy Right Now?
author: Catie Hogan
description: Rigetti Computing's stock has fallen from an October 2025 all-time high of $58 to around $21, raising questions about its investment potential. The quantum computing company received up to $100 million in U.S. government funding and tripled Q1 2026 revenue to $4.4 million, but remains unprofitable with significant operating losses. With $400+ million in cash, Rigetti has runway to develop commercially viable quantum applications, though success depends on long-term market adoption and faces intense competition from pure-play quantum companies and tech giants like Google and IBM.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company shows promising fundamentals with government backing, revenue growth, and strong cash position, but remains unprofitable with highly speculative long-term prospects dependent on unproven quantum computing commercial applications. High volatility and competitive threats warrant cautious outlook.

title: Xanadu Quantum vs. IonQ: The Better Quantum Computing Stock Buy for 2026
author: Robert Izquierdo
description: The article compares two quantum computing companies: Xanadu Quantum Technologies, a newly public photonic quantum computing company, and IonQ, which uses ion-based technology. While both show strong revenue growth, IonQ is recommended as the better investment due to significantly higher sales ($64.7M vs $2.8M in Q1), larger cash reserves ($3.1B vs $272.5M), and a more attractive valuation (P/S ratio of 98 vs 700). IonQ's technology has gained more market traction and it has assembled a comprehensive technology stack positioning it for sustained growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IonQ is recommended as the superior investment choice. The company demonstrates significantly higher revenue ($64.7M Q1 2026, 755% YoY growth), substantially larger cash reserves ($3.1B), better market traction, a more attractive valuation (P/S of 98), and a comprehensive technology stack. Despite higher operating losses due to acquisitions, the company's strong fundamentals and growth trajectory support a positive outlook.

IonQ is recommended as the superior investment choice. The company demonstrates significantly higher revenue ($64.7M Q1 2026, 755% YoY growth), substantially larger cash reserves ($3.1B), better market traction, a more attractive valuation (P/S of 98), and a comprehensive technology stack. Despite higher operating losses due to acquisitions, the company's strong fundamentals and growth trajectory support a positive outlook.

Xanadu shows promise as a newcomer with innovative photonic quantum technology, strong partnerships (AMD), and impressive revenue growth (4x increase to $2.8M). However, it is not recommended over IonQ due to significantly lower revenue, smaller cash reserves ($272.5M), much higher valuation (P/S of 700), and less proven market traction. The company remains viable but faces competitive disadvantages.

AMD is mentioned as a strategic partner of Xanadu Quantum Technologies. While the partnership indicates confidence in Xanadu's technology, AMD itself is not the focus of the analysis and no specific sentiment drivers related to AMD are discussed in the article.

title: 2 Quantum Stocks Are Drawing Capital as AI Infrastructure Hits a Wall
author: Jeffrey Neal Johnson
description: Capital is shifting from AI infrastructure to quantum computing as classical computing faces energy constraints and scalability limits. IonQ and Quantum Computing Inc. are leading the transition with record revenues, aggressive M&A activity, and government backing, though extreme volatility driven by short squeezes and insider selling characterizes these high-beta stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Record Q1 2026 revenue of $64.7M (755% YoY growth), raised full-year guidance to $260-270M, $470M in remaining performance obligations indicating long-term enterprise contracts, acquired SkyWater for $1.8B for vertical integration, secured DARPA HARQ program spot, and benefits from $2B federal quantum funding allocation.

Record Q1 2026 revenue of $64.7M (755% YoY growth), raised full-year guidance to $260-270M, $470M in remaining performance obligations indicating long-term enterprise contracts, acquired SkyWater for $1.8B for vertical integration, secured DARPA HARQ program spot, and benefits from $2B federal quantum funding allocation.

Executed $110M acquisition of Luminar Semiconductor, Q1 revenue surged 9364% to $3.69M, following same vertical integration playbook as IonQ, benefits from federal government backing and defense sector demand, though carries higher short interest (32.6%) creating volatility.

Unveiled Majorana 2 quantum processor with 12 topological qubits and 1,000-fold reliability increase, demonstrating viable scalable quantum hardware and establishing macroeconomic floor that de-risks the broader quantum sector for institutional investors.

title: Buy, Sell, or Hold: Where 5 of Wall Street's Hottest Stocks Stand Right Now
author: Justin Pope
description: The article evaluates five hot tech stocks: Nvidia and ServiceNow are recommended as buys due to compelling valuations and strong growth prospects; Figma is also a buy candidate after a significant decline made it more attractive; IonQ is rated a sell due to an unjustifiably high valuation relative to its business; Netflix is rated a hold as the company faces uncertainty despite its market leadership.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite strong business fundamentals including $3.1B cash and raised revenue guidance, the stock trades at over 77 times sales with a $21B market cap, making it one of the most expensive stocks on the market and difficult to justify for a business that still has much to prove.

Despite strong business fundamentals including $3.1B cash and raised revenue guidance, the stock trades at over 77 times sales with a $21B market cap, making it one of the most expensive stocks on the market and difficult to justify for a business that still has much to prove.

Trading at 23 times earnings with expected 52% annual earnings growth over the next 3-5 years, the valuation is compelling. CEO projects $1 trillion in orders for Blackwell and Vera Rubin by 2027, signaling continued AI boom momentum.

Despite a 56% stock decline, the company has pivoted to embrace AI and targets $30B annual subscription revenue by 2030 (up from $15.7B guidance for 2026). Trading at less than 25 times earnings, it could be a bargain if growth targets are met.

After dropping 84% from its IPO high, the stock now trades at just over 8 times trailing revenue. The business is thriving with 46% revenue growth acceleration and 139% net revenue retention, making it a safer bet with strong return potential.

While the company remains a streaming leader with expansion into live sports, recent stock decline, co-founder departure, and uncertainty about long-term growth prospects warrant caution. Stock trades below 52-week highs, suggesting it may be wise to wait before investing.
